title: beringeria cinerea
side_effects: Beringeria cinerea can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and even liver damage. Some individuals may also experience headaches, dizziness, and fatigue after consuming Beringeria cinerea in excessive amounts. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can also cause digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, in sensitive individuals.
